sections.paragraph:
δεκατόω pf. δεδεκάτωκα, pass. δεδεκάτωμαι (s. prec. entry; 2 Esdr 20:38; Dositheus 77, 7; Suda in conjunction w. Hb 7:9) collect, receive tithes τινά fr. someone Hb 7:6. Pass. in act. sense pay tithes vs. 9.—DELG s.v. δέκα; M-M.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

prec. prec. = preceding

Dositheus Dositheus , gramm., noted for a bilingual work: Ars Grammatica, IV? a.d.—List 5

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

act. act. = active

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 217
